it.<|0.22|><|endoftext|> <|startoftranscript|><|en|><|transcribe|><|0.00|> Both of the readings this morning from Leviticus and from Matthew<|7.00|> <|7.00|> exhort God's people to love your neighbor three-year cycle of readings.<|24.00|> <|24.00|> So that tells you that this message is everywhere.<|28.60|> <|startoftranscript|><|en|><|transcribe|><|0.00|> in Scripture.<|2.38|> <|2.38|> There are James.<|20.24|> <|20.24|> Perhaps there is a reason it has to be said so many times.<|26.30|> <|startoftranscript|><|en|><|transcribe|><|0.00|> Loving our neighbor isn't always easy.<|5.00|> <|5.00|> We are a week and a half away from an historic election in our country.<|13.00|> <|13.00|> So much is in turmoil.<|16.00|> <|16.00|> Our economy, our social order, our very health.<|20.40|> <|startoftranscript|><|en|><|transcribe|><|0.00|> November 3rd will be a moment in which we each have a chance to decide how we will love our neighbor on that day and in the days following.<|11.12|> <|12.12|> I offer that the uptick in searches for schadenfreude on October 2nd is a warning to us.<|21.84|> <|startoftranscript|><|en|><|transcribe|><|0.00|> to all pray about how we can best guard All your heart, all your soul, all your mind.<|6.48|> <|7.28|> When we commit all of ourselves to loving the Lord our God with everything we are and everything we have,<|19.00|> <|startoftranscript|><|en|><|transcribe|><|0.00|> loving our neighbor will flow out of that.<|4.60|> <|5.54|> When all we have is focused on loving God,<|9.30|> <|9.72|> there is no space for finding joy in another's misfortune.<|15.18|> <|15.18|> There is only the clear knowledge that our well-being is intimately tied up<|23.14|> <|23.14|> in the well-being of every other, every neighbor.<|28.02|> <|startoftranscript|><|en|><|transcribe|><|0.00|> As we move through the coming weeks, there will be some who are delighted by the outcome of the election and some who will be dismayed no matter who wins or loses.<|11.66|> <|13.32|> Take a deep breath.<|14.72|> <|startoftranscript|><|en|><|transcribe|><|0.00|> -<|1.28|><|endoftext|> <|startoftranscript|><|en|><|transcribe|><|0.00|> that neighbor you are struggling to tolerate,<|3.58|> <|4.70|> let God love them through you,<|6.46|> <|7.46|> focusing all your heart, mind, and soul on your love of God.<|14.40|> <|15.78|> Everything flows from that.<|17.76|> <|19.66|> Be well, my friends.<|21.34|> <|21.78|> I miss you.<|22.90|> <|startoftranscript|><|en|><|transcribe|><|0.00|> Thank you.<|1.20|><|endoftext|>

<|startoftranscript|><|en|><|transcribe|><|0.00|> On October 2nd, we received news that our president was diagnosed with COVID-19.<|7.76|>

<|startoftranscript|><|en|><|transcribe|><|0.00|> Both of the readings this morning from Leviticus and from Matthew<|7.00|>

<|startoftranscript|><|en|><|transcribe|><|0.00|> My friends, the commandment is not to love only those who love us, nor is it to only love the neighbors we find lovable.<|12.32|>
